name: ExtensionDetectedIssue_Encounter
title: R5: Encounter detected issue is part of (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `DetectedIssue.encounter` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`DetectedIssue.encounter` 0..1 `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Encounter)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `DetectedIssue.encounter` 0..1 `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Encounter)`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `DetectedIssue.encounter` has a context of DetectedIssue based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `DetectedIssue`.
Element `DetectedIssue.encounter` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Encounter detected issue is part of (new)
element.comment:
Element `DetectedIssue.encounter` has a context of DetectedIssue based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `DetectedIssue`.
Element `DetectedIssue.encounter` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
This will typically be the encounter the DetectedIssue was created during, but some DetectedIssues may be initiated prior to or after the official completion of an encounter but still be tied to the context of the encounter (e.g. pre-admission lab tests).

element.short: Encounter detected issue is part of
element.definition: The encounter during which this issue was detected.
element.comment: This will typically be the encounter the DetectedIssue was created during, but some DetectedIssues may be initiated prior to or after the official completion of an encounter but still be tied to the context of the encounter (e.g. pre-admission lab tests).
